programs.name: Choices for Care (CFC) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: The Choices for Care waiver provides long-term services and supports to eligible seniors needing nursing home level of care. It helps avoid or delay nursing home placement by offering home and community-based services.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Vermont seniors requiring nursing home level care who meet income and functional eligibility criteria.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Long-term services and supports to remain in community settings instead of nursing homes.[1]

programs.name: Medicaid for Aged, Blind, and Disabled (ABD)
programs.what_it_does: Provides health coverage for seniors and disabled individuals. Serves as the basis for Medicare Savings Programs eligibility in Vermont.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+, blind, or disabled Vermonters meeting income and resource limits (e.g., $2,000 individual/$3,000 couple for SSI-related).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive Medicaid health coverage including long-term care services.[1][4]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Not explicitly named in Vermont-specific results; federally administered but availability unclear from sources. Provides comprehensive medical and social services to eligible frail elderly.
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home eligible seniors 55+ who can live safely in community.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitated care including adult day health and home care.

programs.name: Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), Qualified Individual (QI)
programs.what_it_does: QMB pays Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, and coinsurance. SLMB and QI help pay Part B premiums for low-income Medicare beneficiaries; Vermont offers three MSPs without resource limits.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Vermont Medicare enrollees 65+ meeting income guidelines, U.S. citizens or legal residents.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Premium, deductible, and coinsurance payments saving hundreds annually.[1]

programs.name: 3SquaresVT for Older Vermonters
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutrition assistance with enhanced rules for seniors 60+, including cash benefits directly deposited into bank accounts if all household members are 65+ or on SSI. Allows deductions for medical expenses like over-the-counter meds, premiums, and home health aids to increase benefit amounts.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Vermonters 60+ or 65+, especially those with medical costs or in all-senior/disabled households; over 17,000 older Vermonters participate monthly.[2]
programs.whats_unique: State administration of SNAP (3SquaresVT) offers cash payments (vs. EBT only) for all-65+ households and up to 3-year recertification periods; medical expense deductions tailored for seniors exceed standard federal SNAP in accessibility.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Monthly food benefits (amount varies by income/household; higher with medical deductions); cash deposit option for eligible seniors.

programs.name: Fuel Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides help with heating costs during winter. Administered through myBenefits portal alongside 3SquaresVT.[2][5]
programs.benefit_value: Cash grants for home heating expenses varying by household size and income.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers home energy efficiency improvements. Included in senior benefits screening tools for utilities.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost home weatherization reducing energy bills.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare enrollment, Advantage/Part D plans, and Medigap. Provides referrals for home care and long-term services via Area Agencies on Aging.[4]

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors. Provided through Area Agencies on Aging and organizations like Senior Solutions.[4][8]
programs.benefit_value: Daily hot meals delivered to support nutrition and independence.

programs.name: Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers support services for family caregivers. Available through senior resource organizations.[7][8]
programs.benefit_value: Respite care, training, and counseling for caregivers.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid training and employment. Federal program accessible via senior benefits screening.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work experience leading to employment.

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal aid for public benefits, housing, and elder rights. Available through VTLawHelp and senior helplines.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Pro bono legal representation and advice.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities. Protects rights in nursing homes and assisted living.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Vermont seniors in long-term care settings.

programs.name: Vermont Senior Companion Program
programs.what_it_does: This statewide program matches volunteers aged 55 and older with older neighbors needing companionship and assistance with daily tasks like grocery shopping and doctor visits. It helps keep seniors in their homes longer, prevents loneliness, and provides respite for family caregivers.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Volunteers aged 55+ with income under $30,120 (single) or $40,880 (couple), serving 10 hours/week; serves older adults needing support.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Statewide volunteer matching program operated by Central Vermont Council on Aging, emphasizing community-based companionship to promote independence; funded through AmeriCorps Seniors but implemented uniquely across Vermont.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Non-monetary: 10 hours/week of free companionship and assistance; stipend for volunteers not specified in detail.

programs.name: Senior Solutions Vet-to-Vet Program
programs.what_it_does: Matches older veterans with veteran volunteers for phone-based companionship, regular chats, and safety check-ins. Provides targeted peer support to reduce isolation among senior vets.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Older veterans in southeastern Vermont seeking companionship.[4]
programs.whats_unique: Veteran-specific peer companionship program run by regional aging agency Senior Solutions, not a standard federal offering; focuses on vet-to-vet matching for emotional support.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Non-monetary: Regular volunteer phone companionship and safety checks.
